inputdata.codenote:
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---

        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---

%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()

inputdata.codenote:
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()

glm-4-airx.modeloutput:
The protocol described in the provided hlpsl file is the SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange) protocol. Below is a description of the protocol based on the content of the hlpsl file. This description should be sufficient to regenerate the hlpsl file for use with AViSPA.

### Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange)

#### General Information:
- **Protocol Name**: SPEKE
- **Variant**: SPEKE with strong password-only authentication
- **Purpose**: This protocol is designed to establish a secure key exchange between two parties (Alice and Bob) using only a shared password as the basis for authentication and key generation.
- **Reference**: http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html
- **Modelers**: Haykal Tej (Siemens CT IC 3, 2003) and Sebastian Mödersheim (ETH Zürich, December 2003)

#### Protocol Participants:
- **Alice (A)**: The initiating party who starts the key exchange process.
- **Bob (B)**: The responding party who completes the key exchange process with Alice.

#### Protocol Messages:
1. **A -> B**: `exp(S(A,B), Na)` - Alice sends Bob the result of exponentiating the shared secret `S(A,B)` with a random number `Na` she generates.
2. **B -> A**: `exp(S(A,B), Nb)` - Bob sends Alice the result of exponentiating the shared secret `S(A,B)` with a random number `Nb` he generates.
3. **A -> B**: `{Ca}_K` - Alice sends Bob a challenge `Ca` encrypted with the key `K` derived from the previous messages.
4. **B -> A**: `{Cb, Ca}_K` - Bob sends Alice the challenge `Ca` and a new challenge `Cb` encrypted with the key `K`.
5. **A -> B**: `{Cb}_K` - Alice sends Bob the challenge `Cb` encrypted with the key `K`.

#### Protocol Roles:
1. **speke_Init**: The role played by Alice, which includes sending the initial message and processing the responses.
2. **speke_Resp**: The role played by Bob, which includes processing the initial message from Alice and responding with the necessary challenges.

#### Protocol States and Transitions:
- **speke_Init**:
  - Initial state: 0, waits for a start signal.
  - Transition 1: Sends `exp(S(A,B), Na)` and moves to state 1.
  - Transition 2: Receives `exp(S(A,B), Nb)` and sends `{Ca}_K`, moving to state 2.
  - Transition 3: Receives `{Cb, Ca}_K` and sends `{Cb}_K`, moving to state 3.
  
- **speke_Resp**:
  - Initial state: 0, waits for the first message from Alice.
  - Transition 1: Receives `exp(S(A,B), Na)` and sends `exp(S(A,B), Nb)`, moving to state 1.
  - Transition 2: Receives `{Ca}_K` and sends `{Cb, Ca}_K`, moving to state 2.
  - Transition 3: Receives `{Cb}_K` and moves to state 3.

#### Protocol Goals:
- **Confidentiality (G12)**: The protocol should ensure the secrecy of the challenges `Ca` and `Cb`, denoted by `sec_i_Ca`, `sec_i_Cb`, `sec_r_Ca`, and `sec_r_Cb`.
- **Message Authentication (G2)**: The protocol should provide message authentication where `SPEKE_Init` authenticates `SPEKE_Resp` on `Cb` and vice versa for `Ca`.

#### Environment Setup:
- The environment includes the intruder and sets up sessions between Alice and Bob, and between Alice and the intruder, and Bob and the intruder, to simulate the protocol and test for security properties.

Based on this description, you would be able to generate the corresponding hlpsl file for the SPEKE protocol. Note that the actual hlpsl syntax, including the correct use of channels, roles, and transitions, must be followed as in the original file to ensure compatibility with AViSPA.

inputdata.codenote:
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---

Using CrypIR constraints and the reference label, the following interpretation keeps the protocol semantics unchanged:

---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---

%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}

%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,

inputdata.codenote:
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()

Of course. As an expert in the AVISPA tool and the HLPSL language, I can provide a detailed description of the provided SPEKE protocol file. This description is structured to be clear and comprehensive, allowing for the regeneration of the HLPSL code from it.

Here is the description of the SPEKE protocol implementation.

***

### Protocol Specification: SPEKE

This document describes the **Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange (SPEKE)** protocol as modeled in HLPSL for analysis by the AVISPA tool.

#### 1. Protocol Overview

*   **Name**: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange)
*   **Purpose**: To allow two parties who only share a (potentially low-entropy) password to negotiate a strong session key and mutually authenticate each other.
*   **Reference**: [Jablon, D. (1996). Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange.](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html)
*   **Security Properties**: The model aims to verify the confidentiality of session-specific values and the mutual authentication between the participants.

#### 2. Protocol Steps (Informal Description)

The protocol consists of a key exchange phase followed by a challenge-response authentication phase. `A` and `B` are the participants, and `S(A,B)` is their shared password.

1.  **Key Exchange**:
    *   `A -> B`: `exp(S(A,B), Na)`
    *   `B -> A`: `exp(S(A,B), Nb)`
    *   Both parties compute the session key `K`.
        *   Alice computes: `K = exp(exp(S(A,B), Nb), Na)`
        *   Bob computes: `K = exp(exp(S(A,B), Na), Nb)`
        *   Due to the properties of modular exponentiation, both compute the same key.

2.  **Authentication**:
    *   `A -> B`: `{Ca}_K` (Alice sends a challenge `Ca`, encrypted with `K`)
    *   `B -> A`: `{Cb, Ca}_K` (Bob proves he decrypted `Ca` and sends his own challenge `Cb`)
    *   `A -> B`: `{Cb}_K` (Alice proves she decrypted `Cb`, completing the authentication)

#### 3. HLPSL Formal Model

The protocol is formally specified with roles for the initiator, responder, a session combining them, and an environment to set up the analysis scenario.

##### 3.1. Role: `speke_Init` (Initiator, played by `A`)

This role describes the actions of the protocol initiator.

*   **Parameters**:
    *   `A`, `B`: Agents (initiator and responder).
    *   `Kab`: Symmetric key (the shared password `S(A,B)`).
    *   `Snd`, `Rcv`: Communication channels.
*   **Local Variables**:
    *   `State`: A number to track the protocol state.
    *   `Na`, `Ca`, `Cb`: Freshly generated nonces/challenges.
    *   `X`, `K`: Intermediate messages.
*   **Transitions**:
    1.  **State 0 -> 1**: Upon receiving a `start` signal, generate a fresh nonce `Na` and send `exp(Kab, Na)` to `B`.
    2.  **State 1 -> 2**: Upon receiving a message `X` (expected to be `exp(Kab, Nb)` from `B`), compute the session key `K` as `exp(X, Na)`. Generate a new challenge `Ca`, send `{Ca}_K` to `B`, and declare `Ca` as a secret shared between `A` and `B`. A `witness` for `Ca` is recorded for authentication purposes.
    3.  **State 2 -> 3**: Upon receiving `{Cb, Ca}_K` from `B` (where `Ca` must match the one sent), send `{Cb}_K` back to `B`. Declare `Cb` as a secret and `request` authentication from `B` on the challenge `Cb`, completing the protocol run.

##### 3.2. Role: `speke_Resp` (Responder, played by `B`)

This role describes the actions of the protocol responder.

*   **Parameters**:
    *   `A`, `B`: Agents (initiator and responder).
    *   `Kab`: Symmetric key (the shared password `S(A,B)`).
    *   `Snd`, `Rcv`: Communication channels.
*   **Local Variables**:
    *   `State`: A number to track the protocol state.
    *   `Nb`, `Cb`, `Ca`: Freshly generated nonces/challenges.
    *   `Y`, `K`: Intermediate messages.
*   **Transitions**:
    1.  **State 0 -> 1**: Upon receiving a message `Y` (expected to be `exp(Kab, Na)` from `A`), generate a fresh nonce `Nb`. Send `exp(Kab, Nb)` to `A` and compute the session key `K` as `exp(Y, Nb)`.
    2.  **State 1 -> 2**: Upon receiving `{Ca}_K` from `A`, generate a new challenge `Cb`. Send `{Cb, Ca}_K` back to `A`. Declare both `Ca` and `Cb` as secrets. Record a `witness` for `Cb` and `request` authentication from `A` on the challenge `Ca`.
    3.  **State 2 -> 3**: Upon receiving `{Cb}_K` from `A` (where `Cb` must match the one sent), the protocol run successfully terminates.

##### 3.3. Role: `session`

This role describes a complete session between two honest participants `A` and `B` using the shared key `Kab`. It achieves this by composing one instance of the `speke_Init` role and one instance of the `speke_Resp` role, running in parallel.

##### 3.4. Role: `environment`

This is the top-level role that defines the overall scenario for the security analysis.

*   **Constants**:
    *   `a`, `b`: Two honest agents.
    *   `kab`: The long-term symmetric key (password) shared between `a` and `b`.
    *   `kai`, `kbi`: Keys shared between `a` and the intruder (`i`), and `i` and `b`, respectively.
    *   `ca`, `cb`: Protocol identifiers for the authentication goals.
*   **Intruder's Initial Knowledge**: The intruder `i` knows the public identities of all agents (`a`, `b`, `i`) and the keys it shares with honest agents (`kai`, `kbi`). The key `kab` is **not** known to the intruder.
*   **Composition**: The environment instantiates three parallel sessions to test for attacks:
    1.  An honest session between `a` and `b`.
    2.  A session between `a` and the intruder `i`.
    3.  A session between the intruder `i` and `b`.

#### 4. Security Goals

The model specifies the following security goals to be verified by AVISPA.

*   **Secrecy**: The challenges `Ca` and `Cb` exchanged during the protocol must remain confidential. The intruder should not be able to learn their values. This is checked using `secrecy_of` on the identifiers defined within the roles.
*   **Mutual Authentication**:
    *   **`authentication_on cb`**: The initiator (`A`) must be assured that it is communicating with the intended responder (`B`). This is verified by checking if `A`'s `request` for the challenge `cb` is matched by `B`'s `witness`.
    *   **`authentication_on ca`**: The responder (`B`) must be assured that it is communicating with the intended initiator (`A`). This is verified by checking if `B`'s `request` for the challenge `ca` is matched by `A`'s `witness`.

[{'role': 'system', 'content': 'You are an expert in the AVISPA tool. I will give you a hlpsl language file of a protocol. Please help me generate the description content of the protocol. The requirement is that based on this description content, you can generate the corresponding hlpsl file that can be run by AVISPA.'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'The content of the hlpsl file is as follows. 
```hlpsl
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()```.'}]

inputdata.codenote:
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---

gpt-4o-mini.modeloutput:
The given HLPSL (High-Level Protocol Specification Language) file describes the SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange) protocol, which is designed for secure key exchange utilizing password-based authentication. 

## Description Content of the SPEKE Protocol

### Protocol Overview
The SPEKE protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, referred to as Alice (A) and Bob (B), using only a shared password as the means of authentication. This protocol leverages strong password authentication to establish a symmetric key (Kab) without transmitting the password itself over the communication channels.

### Variant
This description specifically covers the variant of the SPEKE protocol that incorporates strong password-only authentication.

### Purpose
The primary aim of the SPEKE protocol is to enable two parties (Alice and Bob) to securely establish a common secret key through a series of message exchanges, even in the presence of an adversary who may eavesdrop or interfere with messages being sent.

### Key Components
- **Shared Password**: The protocol relies on a shared password (S(A,B)), known beforehand to both Alice and Bob.
- **Nonce Values**: Each participant generates a unique nonce (Na for Alice and Nb for Bob) to ensure the freshness of the communication and prevent replay attacks.
- **Message Structure**: The key exchange consists of multiple messages exchanged between Alice and Bob, which are encrypted using the derived key (K) that is based on the shared password and the nonces.

### Protocol Steps
1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing the result of an exponential operation using the shared password and her nonce (Na).
   - Bob replies with a similar operation using Alice's message and his nonce (Nb).

2. **Challenge/Response Mechanism**:
   - Alice and Bob exchange challenge messages (Ca and Cb respectively) that are tied to their derived key (K).
   - These messages contribute to verifying that each participant possesses the correct shared password without revealing the password itself.

3. **Authentication**:
   - Alice and Bob authenticate each other based on the challenges they send to one another.
   - The successful exchange of challenges ensures that both parties are indeed who they claim to be and have the correct shared password.

### Roles
- **Alice Role (speke_Init)**: Responsible for initiating the protocol and sending the first message along with nonce and challenge generation.
- **Bob Role (speke_Resp)**: Responsible for responding to Alice's messages, generating his nonce and challenges, and sending replies to Alice.

### Security Goals
The SPEKE protocol aims to achieve the following security properties:
- **Confidentiality**: Ensure that the challenge messages (Ca, Cb) remain secret from any eavesdroppers (including an intruder). 
- **Authentication**: Verify the identities of both parties. Specifically, Alice must authenticate Bob, and Bob must authenticate Alice through their respective challenge messages.

### Summary
The SPEKE protocol is a powerful mechanism for secure password-based authentication and key exchange. By employing nonces and challenge-response techniques, it mitigates risks associated with password transmission and establishes robust mutual authentication. The effectiveness of the protocol is guaranteed under the assumption that the shared password remains secret and resilient against brute-force attacks.

### References
The specification of the SPEKE protocol, alongside its formal model, can be found in the works of Jablon et al. and various contributions from Haykal Tej and Sebastian Mödersheim.

### Limitations and Known Problems
Currently, there are no inherent limitations noted within the protocol, and no known attacks have been identified that compromise its security.

---

This detailed description can serve as the basis for understanding the SPEKE protocol and can be translated back to an HLPSL specification for modeling and analysis using the AVISPA tools.

```hlpsl
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
